items.title_cn: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.

items.summary_cn: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

items.title_cn: Show HN: Smart model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
items.title_en: Show HN: Smart model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
items.summary_cn:
We built a model router that plugs into coding agents (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and intelligently sends requests to the best model to serve them. Here's a quick demo of running it locally: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=isKhAyivtfM.

At Weave, we write most of our code with AI, and it's been getting more expensive. This came to a head when Opus 4.7 was released and, th
items.summary_en:
We built a model router that plugs into coding agents (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and intelligently sends requests to the best model to serve them. Here's a quick demo of running it locally: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=isKhAyivtfM.

items.title_cn: Show HN: Hacker News on a train station-style flip board
items.title_en: Show HN: Hacker News on a train station-style flip board
items.summary_cn:
Although the page itself is more just fun to have made and look at (I like the flip sound), the fun part is how I made it to verify the (and I hate to say it) vibe host service I've been working on. The recent flip board back and forth's on Twitter (X) are what inspired me.

The idea here is that people (like me or you) can create something neat like this, and others can remix it, change it and publish their own version. This is that all in action and it worked great. I wrote a blog a
